Scenario.title: The Cutting Edge Campaign: Water Treatment Disruption
Scenario.overview: Water and Wastewater Systems Complex The Cutting Edge campaign aims to disrupt water treatment processes by targeting key personnel and exploiting vulnerabilities in SCADA systems.

threat_actor_profile.motivation: Financial gain through ransomware and data extortion

Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Shared DB or directory, Web server services such as emails and databases, Shadow IT, Poor implementation of access controls, Authentication issues
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Web, Phishing, Vulnerability exploit, Brute-force attack, Credential compromise

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The Cutting Edge group begins by gathering information about target water treatment facilities, their infrastructure, and personnel through open-source intelligence.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 673 - Developer Signing Maliciously Altered Software
stage.description: The group develops a custom exploit for an unknown vulnerability in a commonly used SCADA system (e.g., Ivanti Connect Secure) to aid in initial access.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2009-3953 [CWE-22] : Microsoft Office Word RTF parsing stack-based buffer overflow
stage.description: The Cutting Edge group targets key personnel at water treatment facilities with a spear-phishing email containing a malicious attachment exploiting the previously developed zero-day vulnerability.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2018-19949 [CWE-502] : Apache Struts2 Remote Code Execution
stage.description: Upon successful execution of the malicious attachment, the group uploads a web shell or backdoor onto the compromised system to establish initial access.

Scenario.summary: The Cutting Edge campaign employs a complex attack strategy involving reconnaissance, resource development, initial access, execution, persistence, privilege escalation, defense evasion, credential access, discovery, lateral movement, collection, exfiltration, and impact to disrupt water treatment processes and potentially cause physical damage.
